race.name: UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki
race.tagline: Greece's inaugural UCI Gran Fondo qualifier combining Mediterranean coastal riding with mountain climbing on the Isthmus of Corinth

vitals.uci_status: UCI Gran Fondo World Series Qualifier

climate.overview: Mediterranean climate in early November. Loutraki is situated on the Gulf of Corinth backed by the Geraneia Mountains, offering coastal conditions with potential for variable weather during the autumn season.

terrain.overview: Mixed terrain combining flat coastal sections with rolling inland climbs. The route features Mediterranean coastal riding along the Saronic Gulf, transitioning to mountain climbing in the interior before returning to sea-level finish.

adventure.explanation: The route offers stunning coastal views along the Saronic Gulf and inland climbs through the scenic Geraneia Mountains and near the Corinth Canal, providing a solid adventure[1][2].

altitude.explanation: Maximum elevation reaches only 300m with total gain around 889-1380m, keeping it relatively low compared to high-mountain gran fondos[1][2].

climate.explanation: November in coastal Loutraki brings mild Mediterranean fall weather, suitable for cycling but with potential for variable conditions[1].

community.explanation: As a new UCI Gran Fondo World Series event, it fosters a welcoming international cycling community for all levels from elites to sportives[2][4].

elevation.explanation: Moderate total elevation gain of 889-1380m over 110-112km features rolling climbs with a max of 300m, challenging but accessible[1][2].

expenses.explanation: High cancellation fees (20-100% depending on timing) and costs for travel, accommodation at the official Club Hotel Casino, plus event fees make it pricey[3].

experience.explanation: Picturesque Peloponnese landscapes, smooth asphalt, winding roads, and UCI organization deliver a memorable, high-quality cycling experience[1][5].

field_depth.explanation: Inclusive categories for elites, masters, juniors, and sportives with age-group podiums ensure a diverse and competitive field[2][4].

length.explanation: The 110-112km distance strikes a balanced gran fondo length, demanding endurance without being excessively long[1][2].

logistics.explanation: Well-organized with bib pickup in Athens or Loutraki, secure bike storage at the official hotel, and clear start/finish at Club Hotel Casino[3].

prestige.explanation: Part of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series with qualification for the finals, organized by municipality and federation, adds solid prestige as a new event[1][4].

race_quality.explanation: UCI-sanctioned with professional organization, safety standards, medals, and podiums ensures high race quality and smooth execution[2][3].

technicality.explanation: Features steep ascents, technical descents, rolling climbs at 3.1% average, and a final small climb for moderate technical demands[1][2].

value.explanation: Offers scenic beauty and UCI prestige, but high fees, travel to Greece, and new-event uncertainties temper overall value[3][5].

biased_opinion.overview: Gran Fondo Loutraki represents an excellent entry point into UCI Gran Fondo World Series racing with genuine competitive value. The inaugural 2025 event attracted 400+ international riders and delivered on its promise of combining Greek coastal beauty with legitimate climbing challenges. The automatic qualification pathway to the 2026 World Championships in Japan elevates its prestige beyond a typical regional event. However, the 112 km distance and moderate 966 m elevation gain position it as an accessible rather than ultra-challenging Gran Fondo, making it ideal for riders seeking international competition without extreme demands.

final_verdict.one_liner: A well-organized UCI World Series qualifier offering Mediterranean scenery and legitimate competition with direct pathway to world championships.
final_verdict.full_verdict: Gran Fondo Loutraki successfully launched as Greece's first UCI Gran Fondo World Series event in November 2025, establishing itself as a quality international cycling event. The 112 km route intelligently balances accessibility with challenge, featuring flat coastal sections that welcome diverse ability levels alongside rolling inland climbs that test fitness. The automatic qualification system for top 25% finishers in each age category provides meaningful competitive stakes. Professional organization by the Municipality of Loutraki and Hellenic Cycling Federation ensures world-class standards. The location's proximity to Athens and established tourism infrastructure supports international participation. For cyclists seeking UCI-sanctioned racing with genuine world championship qualification opportunities, combined with Mediterranean scenery and strong community atmosphere, Loutraki delivers exceptional value. The event's inaugural success and integration into the prestigious UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendar positions it as a must-do qualifier for ambitious Gran Fondo racers.

logistics.overview: Loutraki is located on the Isthmus of Corinth approximately 1 hour from Athens. The event is organized by the Municipality of Loutraki in collaboration with KYVERNITIS Travel and sanctioned by the Hellenic Cycling Federation. Start and finish occur at Club Hotel Casino Loutraki, which provides secure bicycle parking.
airports: Athens International Airport (ATH) - approximately 60 km away
logistics.accommodation: Loutraki is a well-established tourist destination with diverse accommodation options. The Club Hotel Casino Loutraki serves as the official start/finish venue. The city offers beaches, thermal spas, restaurants, and nightlife facilities.
logistics.registration_notes: Registrations are personal, final, and non-transferable. Cancellation policy applies tiered refunds: 0-90 days before event (20% fee), 89-60 days (50% fee), 59-15 days (80% fee), 14-0 days (100% fee). Registration includes official cycling jersey, professionally marked closed route, timing services, feed stations, technical support, medical assistance, finisher's medal, and UCI medals for top 25% in each age category.

history.overview: Gran Fondo Loutraki was organized for the first time in 2025, establishing Greece's inaugural UCI Gran Fondo World Series event. The event is part of the prestigious UCI Gran Fondo World Series promoting 'Cycling for All' with inclusive participation across elite, junior, master, and sportive categories.
history.founder: Municipality of Loutraki in collaboration with KYVERNITIS Travel, with technical support from the Hellenic Cycling Federation (EOP)
notable_moments: 2025: November 2 - Inaugural UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki held with 400+ riders from 25 countries
Women's race winner was a 9x Greek National Champion with winning time of 2:53:42
Top 25% finishers in each UCI age/gender category automatically qualified for August 2026 UCI Gran Fondo World Championships in Niseko, Japan
Event successfully integrated into UCI Gran Fondo World Series calendar as official qualifier

course_description.character: Mediterranean Gran Fondo combining coastal and mountain terrain. The route transitions from flat seaside riding along the Saronic Gulf through inland rolling climbs in the Peloponnese interior, returning to coastal finish. Characterized by scenic beauty, moderate climbing, and well-marked professional course management.

suffering_zones.label: Two-part inland climb to 300 m elevation with 3.1% average gradient - sustained but manageable climbing challenge
Final 10 km includes small climb approaching finish - late-race effort test

course_description.signature_challenge: The two-part inland climb transitioning from coastal flats to interior mountains, offering sustained rolling gradient (3.1% average) with maximum elevation of 300 m above sea level. Provides legitimate climbing test while remaining accessible to diverse ability levels.

guide_variables.best_for: Intermediate to advanced cyclists seeking UCI-sanctioned competition with world championship qualification opportunity. Suitable for riders comfortable with 112 km distance and 966 m elevation gain.
guide_variables.key_attractions: Mediterranean coastal scenery along Saronic Gulf, Geraneia Mountains backdrop, proximity to ancient Corinth archaeological sites, Corinth Canal engineering marvel, Loutraki thermal springs and casino entertainment

non_negotiables: UCI Gran Fondo World Series sanctioned event with automatic qualification to 2026 World Championships for top 25% finishers
Professional organization with feed stations, technical support, medical assistance, and timing services
Closed, professionally marked course with neutral support vehicles
Mediterranean coastal and mountain terrain combination
Accessible location approximately 1 hour from Athens
International field with 400+ participants from 25 countries in inaugural 2025 event

citations.title: Loutraki - UCI Gran Fondo World Series
UCI Gran Fondo Greece Loutraki 2025 - KYVERNITIS Travel
Greek first UCI Gran Fondo Loutraki impresses

research_metadata.data_completeness: High - inaugural event with comprehensive official documentation available
research_metadata.information_gaps: GPS coordinates and specific registration pricing not provided in sources. Detailed weather data for November not available. Specific accommodation partnerships beyond Club Hotel Casino Loutraki not documented.
research_metadata.source_quality: Excellent - official UCI Gran Fondo World Series website, event organizer documentation, and race reports from participants
